source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Explosion at Enchilada Platform, Garden Banks Block 128

event.top_event: Explosion and Fire at Pig Launcher
actions_taken: Medical evacuation of two injured operators to Galveston, TX
Third-party fire safety team boarded platform to verify valve positions
Closure of open valves on November 9-10, 2017
event.summary: On November 8, 2017 at 1:18 a.m., an explosion occurred at the 30-inch sales gas pipeline pig launcher on the Enchilada platform (GB Block 128) operated by Shell Offshore Inc. Two operators were preparing to launch a pig when the pig launcher closure door failed explosively, propelling the door and other items overboard. A fire ensued, fed by natural gas from the pipeline. One operator sustained concussion, back injuries, and second-degree burns; the other suffered minor injuries. All 46 platform personnel were safely evacuated. The root cause was incomplete closure of the clamp rings due to procedural failures in securing the closure door, combined with inadequate hazard analysis, improper management of change, and insufficient supervision.
recommendations: GOMR Office of Safety Management should issue a Safety Alert/Bulletin emphasizing adherence to established procedures, including lockout/tagout
GOMR Office of Safety Management should perform a SEMS program evaluation of GB-128 'A' facility in areas of Hazard Analysis, Operating Procedures, Safe Work Practices, and Mechanical Integrity

hazards.name: Pressurized natural gas in pig launcher system
Mechanical failure of pig launcher closure assembly
Improper isolation of pig launcher from sales gas pipeline

threats.name: Incomplete closure of clamp rings due to improper ratchet operation
Failure to follow lockout/tagout procedures on isolation valves
Inadequate verification of pig launcher isolation before pressurization
Procedural deviation in side ratchet binder operation
Defective pigging valve (HCV-9131) unable to maintain positive isolation
Kicker valve (SDV-9133) not properly locked out and opening during incident

consequences.name: Explosive opening of pig launcher closure door
Fire from ignition of released gas

controls.name: Approved Operating Procedure for Pig Loading (KAH-9310)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure safe sequencing of pig loading operations
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent improper isolation and closure

supporting_text: Step 26 of the OP was where the operators were supposed to 'close & secure receiver door.' The OP listed a process safety advisory, which stated, 'When closing the door, use the [side] gear ratchet and do not over tighten. LOOSEN [SIDE] RATCHET WHEN CLAMP IS TIGHTENED.'
The operator who closed the door stated that he fully tightened the side ratchet, but did not loosen or disengage the side ratchet binder from the closure door.
Neither OP-1 nor OP-2 stated that they conducted a visual inspection to ensure the clamp ring was properly secured.

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout (LO/TO) of isolation valves
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/administrative control to prevent inadvertent operation of isolation valves
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement to prevent loss of isolation

supporting_text: OP-1 did not lockout/tagout (LO/TO) any of these valves.
The BSEE investigation team saw no indication that a LO tag was applied to the SDV-9133 HLR.
The BSEE investigation team saw no indication that a LO tag was applied to SDV-9131, although the team discovered a LO device resting on the valve actuator during its site visit.
The BSEE investigation team saw no indication that any LO tags were applied to either of these valves.
The failure to lock out the 12-inch 'kicker' valve may have resulted in the valve opening at some point following the explosion.

controls.name: Pressure isolation verification (bleed-down test)
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to verify pig launcher is isolated from sales gas pipeline
controls.lod_basis: Procedural step to confirm isolation before opening closure door

supporting_text: Upon opening the ½-inch vent valve and the ¾-inch trap drain valves, OP-1 discovered that gas was coming out of the drain, indicating that the PL was not properly isolated from the sales gas.

In an attempt to achieve positive isolation, OP-2 cycled HCV-9131 open and closed. At 12:23 a.m., OP-2 opened the 2-inch flare line to bleed the PL, but was unable to achieve positive isolation. After two subsequent attempts, OP-2 was able to obtain a positive seal on HCV-9131 at 1:00 a.m.

Step 14 of the OP stated the following: 'Close 2-inch flare valve. Allow pig trap to sit for 10 minutes. If there is any pressure build up, equalize the trap and re-actuate HCV-9131 and repeat bleed down (Step 12). Repeat 10 minute sit test. Repeat above steps until a positive isolation is established.'

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work practices before task execution
controls.lod_basis: SEMS requirement for activities identified in Integrated Job Planning Matrix

supporting_text: SOI's PtW Matrix specified that a JSA was required for this task, yet the OP did not indicate the requirement for a JSA.
The BSEE investigation team received no indication that a JSA was performed prior to commencing work in this instance.
The OP contained in their Integrated Job Planning Matrix met the criteria for conducting a JSA.

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) process
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to review and approve procedural changes before implementation
controls.lod_basis: Organizational process to prevent uncontrolled changes to procedures

supporting_text: About one year prior, maintenance was performed on the HCV-9131 valve due to excessive leaking. Contract personnel discovered that the valve would not consistently seal. The valve was neither replaced nor repaired; instead, the procedure was amended in order to ensure that the valve eventually created a seal prior to opening the PL closure door.
The BSEE investigation team received no indication that the MOC process was followed prior to this procedural change being implemented.

controls.name: Supervisory oversight and control of work
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure procedures are followed and work is performed safely
controls.lod_basis: Management responsibility to ensure safe work practices

supporting_text: The BSEE investigation team concluded that the excessive number of procedural discrepancies indicated a lack of sufficient supervisory control over the work performed in this instance.
Both rotating PICs approved the job procedure, eliminating SOI-mandated risk assessment controls by not requiring a JSA to be conducted.
SOI normally has three operators on duty during the night shift at Enchilada, but the third operator departed Enchilada early to attend training onshore.
Since they were the only personnel performing operations work during this shift, they did not flag off the area around the PL.

controls.name: Pressure warning lock installation and verification
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to verify proper alignment of clamp rings before pressurization
controls.lod_basis: OEM requirement to ensure clamp ring alignment

supporting_text: After actuating the top ratchet, OP-2 stated that he inserted the pressure warning lock (bleed pin). Neither OP-1 nor OP-2 stated that they conducted a visual inspection to ensure the clamp ring was properly secured.
The pressure-warning lock was not installed through both clamp ring lugs, confirming improper alignment of the clamp rings.

OEM operation instruction listed the following: 'Where installed, disengage the [side] ratchet binder from the closure door. WARNING: Make sure door, clamp rings, and pressure-warning lock are fully closed and secured and the [side] ratchet binder is unhooked from the closure door before attempting to pressurize vessel.'

controls.name: Gas detector monitoring (ASH-RX-E1-1)
controls.barrier_role: Detection control to identify gas release during pig launcher operations
controls.lod_basis: Safety system to detect gas release before ignition

supporting_text: OP-1 was in the control room, and began monitoring the gas detector (tag number ASH-RX-E1-1).
OP-2 proceeded to open the PL closure door and notified OP-1 that the pig was ready to be loaded.

controls.name: Staffing requirements (three operators for pig loading)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure adequate personnel for safe task execution
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement based on risk assessment

supporting_text: The written operating procedure (OP) was approved by both rotating PICs, and stated the required 'Man-hours by craft' at three operators for 45 minutes.
SOI normally has three operators on duty during the night shift at Enchilada, but the third operator departed Enchilada early to attend training onshore.

With the stated objective being to safely load a 30-inch gas pig without risk to personnel or asset, the SOI procedure specifies a total of three (3) operators for the work. In this instance, two operators completed the work.

controls.name: Mechanical integrity of pigging valve (HCV-9131)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to maintain isolation capability of critical valve
controls.lod_basis: Equipment maintenance to ensure functional isolation

supporting_text: About one year prior, maintenance was performed on the HCV-9131 valve due to excessive leaking. Contract personnel discovered that the valve would not consistently seal.
The valve was neither replaced nor repaired; instead, the procedure was amended in order to ensure that the valve eventually created a seal prior to opening the PL closure door.

controls.name: Platform evacuation and muster procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to safely evacuate personnel following incident
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response procedure to minimize personnel consequences

supporting_text: Both operators managed to make their way over to the Auxiliary Control Center (Aux MCC) as a muster point.
OP-2 contacted the operator shore base and advised them that the 'trap door blew off,' and to 'shut everyone else in.'
All 46 personnel safely evacuated the platform and proceeded to a nearby platform.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
